items.title: Show HN: Effort Router: Intelligent /effort selection per Claude turn
items.why_it_matters: Claude Code doesn't natively support dynamic effort levels, meaning you often burn expensive high-effort tokens on simple tasks. Effort Router dynamically adjusts Claude's effort per conversation turn, saving you tokens on easy queries while scaling up for complex refactors. This week, install the router and run your next AI-ingest coding session through it to see how much it cuts your API bill.

items.title: Show HN: I built claw-coder which is the first atonomous local AI agent
items.why_it_matters: A new CLI tool called claw-coder has launched as a local-first, autonomous coding agent alternative to Claude Code. It runs code execution inside secure Docker containers to prevent accidental damage to your system while still giving the agent full capability to test and build. This week, run `npm install -g claw-coder` and ask it to write a simple utility function for your Supabase backend to compare its speed to Claude Code.

items.title: Show HN: Amnesia – audit Claude Code's memory for contradictions
items.why_it_matters: As you use Claude Code, its persistent memory can accumulate contradictory instructions that confuse the agent and degrade its output quality. Amnesia is a new tool that audits Claude Code's memory files, letting you find and clear out these contradictions. This week, clone the Amnesia repo and run it on your AI-ingest workspace to see exactly what Claude Code has memorized about your project.
